understanding.project_summary: This project provides a Wardrobe Recommendation Engine that builds searchable wardrobe metadata and embeddings, stores them in PostgreSQL + pgvector, and exposes a CLI-driven interactive fashion chatbot and a set of utility scripts for ingestion, embedding generation, migration, and search. It uses OpenAI for captioning, query understanding and LLM reranking, CLIP/transformers for visual embeddings, and a pgvector-enabled Postgres database for hybrid retrieval.
understanding.architecture_overview: The application is organized into three main layers: 1) Data ingestion & embedding pipelines (scripts, src.engine.captioner, src.engine.embeddings, src.engine.visual_embeddings) which generate caption metadata, text embeddings and visual embeddings and write JSON files under data/; 2) Persistence & retrieval (src.db.pgvector_store, scripts.migrate_json_to_pgvector, src.engine.pgvector_similar_search) which create pgvector schema, migrate JSON embeddings into Postgres and perform text/visual/hybrid searches; 3) Orchestration & application logic (main.py, src.engine.fashion_assistant, src.engine.fashion_chat_assistant, src.engine.outfit_recommender, src.engine.similar_search, src.engine.hybrid_retrieval) which implement query understanding, candidate building, BM25 reranking, LLM calls for outfit creation and a ChatMemory adapter backed by Postgres (src.db.postgres_chat_memory / src.engine.chat_memory). Utilities and small helpers (src.utils, scripts/*) support file paths, image handling and CLI entrypoints. OpenAI and Transformer clients are used directly from many engine modules and are configured via environment variables (.env).

execution_flow.title: Environment and dependency checks
execution_flow.description: main() validates environment (.env) for OPENAI_API_KEY and DATABASE_URL, ensures project folders exist and prints startup headers.

execution_flow.title: Uploaded images validation and inventory creation
execution_flow.description: main() ensures uploaded wardrobe images exist. If local inventory/metadata/embeddings are missing, scripts.check_uploaded_images and scripts.ingest_sample_closet are used to create wardrobe_inventory.json and wardrobe_metadata.json.

execution_flow.title: Text embedding generation
execution_flow.description: scripts.run_api or src.engine.embeddings.generate_embeddings_from_metadata creates text embeddings (data/wardrobe_embeddings.json) using OpenAI embedding model.

execution_flow.title: Visual embedding generation and hybrid file building
execution_flow.description: scripts.build_hybrid_embeddings / src.engine.visual_embeddings add visual embeddings to text embedding items and produce data/wardrobe_hybrid_embeddings.json.

execution_flow.title: pgvector migration and DB readiness
execution_flow.description: main() checks pgvector DB reachability (get_item_count). If DB is empty, scripts.migrate_json_to_pgvector migrates wardrobe_hybrid_embeddings.json into PostgreSQL and creates pgvector schema and indexes.

execution_flow.title: Chat memory schema and session setup
execution_flow.description: main() creates chat memory tables using create_chat_memory_schema and creates Postgres-backed chat memory (chat_sessions, chat_messages, conversation_state). ChatMemory adapters ensure per-session state for follow-ups.

execution_flow.title: Start interactive fashion assistant
execution_flow.description: main() constructs FashionChatAssistant which drives the chat loop: understanding user queries, merging context, running hybrid retrieval / similar searches and invoking LLMs for outfit generation and stylist notes.

Create PostgreSQL connection.

functions.name: create_pgvector_schema
functions.docstring: Create pgvector extension and wardrobe_items table.
called_functions_preview: cur.execute('CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;')

cur.execute('
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_items (
                    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
                    item_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
                    filename TEXT,
                    image_path TEXT,
                    caption TEXT,
                    search_text TEXT,
                    category JSONB,
                    color JSONB,
                    style JSONB,
                    item_type TEXT,
                    text_embedding vector(1536),
                    visual_embedding vector(512),
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_item_type_idx
                ON wardrobe_items (item_type);
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_text_embedding_idx
                ON wardrobe_items
                USING ivfflat (text_embedding vector_cosine_ops)
                WITH (lists = 100);
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_visual_embedding_idx
                ON wardrobe_items
                USING ivfflat (visual_embedding vector_cosine_ops)
                WITH (lists = 100);
                ')

                INSERT INTO wardrobe_items (

functions.name: create_chat_memory_schema
functions.docstring:
Create production-style chat memory tables in PostgreSQL.

Tables:
- chat_sessions: one row per chat session
- chat_messages: all user/assistant messages
- conversation_state: latest useful memory for follow-up handling
called_functions_preview:
cur.execute('
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_sessions (
                    session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                    user_id TEXT,
                    title TEXT,
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ')

cur.execute("
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
                    message_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES chat_sessions(session_id)
                    ON DELETE CASCADE,
                    role TEXT NOT NULL,
                    content TEXT NOT NULL,
                    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ")

cur.execute("
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS conversation_state (
                    session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES chat_sessions(session_id)
                    ON DELETE CASCADE,
                    last_intent TEXT,
                    last_item_id TEXT,
                    last_image_path TEXT,
                    last_occasion TEXT,

risks_and_gaps.title: No authentication or access controls
risks_and_gaps.description: The system relies on local CLI entrypoints and Postgres credentials provided via DATABASE_URL in .env. There is no authentication, authorization, or API gateway for multi-user or networked deployment.

risks_and_gaps.title: Secrets and environment management
risks_and_gaps.description: OpenAI API keys and DATABASE_URL are read from a local .env; there is no explicit secret management guidance or runtime checks beyond presence, nor guidance on rotating keys or limiting access.

risks_and_gaps.title: Limited error handling and retries for external services
risks_and_gaps.description: OpenAI calls, transformer/CLIP model loading, and DB operations use direct calls without robust retry/backoff or centralized error classification. Long-running pipelines may fail silently or produce partial results.

risks_and_gaps.title: No automated tests or CI indicators
risks_and_gaps.description: Repository scan shows no tests or test harness. Critical flows (embedding generation, DB migration, LLM prompt parsing) lack unit/integration tests that would ensure robustness during refactors.

risks_and_gaps.title: Observability and logging are basic
risks_and_gaps.description: Modules use print statements for progress and debug output rather than a structured logging framework, making production debugging and log aggregation harder.

risks_and_gaps.title: Synchronous/blocking calls present
risks_and_gaps.description: All LLM, embedding and model inference calls are synchronous; long-running tasks block the CLI and are not exposed as background jobs. No task queue / async orchestration is present.

risks_and_gaps.title: Deployment and scalability guidance missing
risks_and_gaps.description: No deployment manifests, Docker Compose, or infra docs in repository. While Docker is suggested for pgvector, there is no recommended deployment architecture for the full stack (GPU/CPU requirements for CLIP, concurrency, scaling LLM calls).

risks_and_gaps.title: Data integrity during migrations
risks_and_gaps.description: Migration to pgvector is manual via scripts.migrate_json_to_pgvector and runs upserts. There is no transactional migration plan, versioning, or idempotent migration steps documented for large datasets.

recommended_document_sections.title: Getting Started & Quickstart
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Step-by-step instructions to run the project locally: prerequisites, .env setup, preparing uploaded images, running the full pipeline (ingest -> embeddings -> hybrid -> migrate) and starting the chat assistant.

recommended_document_sections.title: Configuration and Secrets
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain required environment variables (OPENAI_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL), recommended secret handling, model selection, and configuration options (embedding models, CLIP model name, weights).

recommended_document_sections.title: Data Pipeline Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document the ingestion and embedding pipeline: scripts.check_uploaded_images, src.engine.captioner, src.engine.embeddings, src.engine.visual_embeddings, and how JSON artifacts (wardrobe_inventory.json, wardrobe_metadata.json, wardrobe_embeddings.json, wardrobe_hybrid_embeddings.json) are produced and used.

recommended_document_sections.title: Database & Migration Guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Instructions to run and configure Postgres + pgvector (including provided Docker command), create schema, migrate JSON to pgvector, and best practices for large migrations and backups.

recommended_document_sections.title: Developer Guide to Recommendation Flows
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain retrieval and ranking flows: hybrid_retrieval (text+visual), pgvector_store SQL queries, BM25 reranking, LLM reranking, and how the outfit_recommender composes candidate pools and LLM prompts.

recommended_document_sections.title: Chat Memory and Session Management
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe ChatMemory abstraction, Postgres-backed conversation_state schema, methods to reset/inspect state, and how follow-ups and context merging work.

recommended_document_sections.title: CLI & Script Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: List available scripts (main.py and scripts/*) with their purpose, expected inputs, example commands and expected outputs to make it easy to run particular tasks independently.

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing, Error Handling and Observability
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guidance on adding unit/integration tests for core modules (pgvector_store, embeddings, captioner), recommended logging framework (structured logs), and patterns for retries and error boundaries for external API calls.

recommended_document_sections.title: Extending & Customizing LLM Prompts
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document key LLM prompts and JSON schemas exchanged with OpenAI in captioner, query_understanding, outfit_recommender and reranker modules, and instructions for modifying prompts or swapping models.

recommended_document_sections.title: Performance & Deployment Notes
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Advice on resource requirements (CPU/GPU for CLIP), concurrency limits for OpenAI calls, suggested deployment approaches (containers, worker queues for long tasks), and scaling pgvector for production.

called_functions_preview: cur.execute('CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;')

cur.execute('
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_items (
                    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
                    item_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
                    filename TEXT,
                    image_path TEXT,
                    caption TEXT,
                    search_text TEXT,
                    category JSONB,
                    color JSONB,
                    style JSONB,
                    item_type TEXT,
                    text_embedding vector(1536),
                    visual_embedding vector(512),
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_item_type_idx
                ON wardrobe_items (item_type);
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_text_embedding_idx
                ON wardrobe_items
                USING ivfflat (text_embedding vector_cosine_ops)
                WITH (lists = 100);
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS wardrobe_visual_embedding_idx
                ON wardrobe_items
                USING ivfflat (visual_embedding vector_cosine_ops)
                WITH (lists = 100);
                ')

functions.name: create_chat_memory_schema
functions.docstring:
Create production-style chat memory tables in PostgreSQL.

Tables:
- chat_sessions: one row per chat session
- chat_messages: all user/assistant messages
- conversation_state: latest useful memory for follow-up handling
called_functions_preview:
cur.execute('
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_sessions (
                    session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                    user_id TEXT,
                    title TEXT,
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ')

cur.execute("
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
                    message_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES chat_sessions(session_id)
                    ON DELETE CASCADE,
                    role TEXT NOT NULL,
                    content TEXT NOT NULL,
                    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ")

cur.execute("
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS conversation_state (
                    session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES chat_sessions(session_id)
                    ON DELETE CASCADE,
                    last_intent TEXT,
                    last_item_id TEXT,
                    last_image_path TEXT,
                    last_occasion TEXT,
                    last_vibe TEXT,
                    last_user_query TEXT,
                    last_assistant_response TEXT,
                    last_recommendation JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
                    context JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
                    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                );
                ")

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages_session_idx
                ON chat_messages (session_id, created_at);
                ')

cur.execute('
                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS chat_sessions_user_idx
                ON chat_sessions (user_id);
                ')
